summary: AI phone receptionist that screens calls via Twilio, with Pipecat orchestration, Groq Llama 3.3 70B for LLM, Deepgram for STT, Fish Audio for TTS, and ElevenLabs as alternate TTS. Includes social engineering detection, caller reputation, spam screening, DND modes, contact sync (Google/HubSpot/iCloud), React frontend with glassmorphic UI, and a Flutter iOS app scaffold. Substantial codebase (~2k+ lines backend services, 20+ commits, 6 phases implemented).

pipecat.details: pipecat-ai[groq,deepgram,twilio] in requirements; GroqLLMService, DeepgramSTTService used in receptionist.py

groq.details: Groq Llama 3.3 70B as primary LLM; used in receptionist + call classifier

fish_audio.details: FishTTSService implemented in fish_tts.py, used as primary TTS in receptionist

deepgram.details: DeepgramSTTService for speech-to-text; also Deepgram Voice Agent API for Phase 3

elevenlabs.details: Alternate TTS provider with voice cloning support

twilio.details: Call handling, media streams, STIR/SHAKEN verification

score_rationale.revenue_potential: Strong B2C SaaS potential — AI call screening is a real pain point. Subscription model ($10-30/mo) with clear value prop. Market timing good with AI voice maturity.
score_rationale.strategic_value: Showcases Pipecat+Groq+Fish Audio stack; differentiates with social engineering detection. Strong demo piece for ForwardLane/SignalHaus AI capabilities.
score_rationale.completeness: Core pipeline (Twilio→Pipecat→Groq→Fish Audio→Deepgram) is wired. 6 phases implemented. But: no end-to-end integration tests, iOS app is scaffold only (0 Dart files), no deployment config beyond Dockerfile, no CI/CD.
score_rationale.urgency: AI call screening market heating up (Truecaller, Hiya, Google Call Screen). First-mover advantage with social engineering detection angle. Ship before commoditized.
score_rationale.effort_remaining: Core services exist but need: real device testing with Twilio, iOS app build-out, deployment pipeline, billing/payments, App Store submission. ~3-5 weeks to MVP.

architecture.frontend: React 19 + Vite + Tailwind + Radix UI + Framer Motion
architecture.ios: Flutter (scaffold only, no Dart files found)
architecture.realtime: Redis pub/sub for call approval, WebSocket for dashboard

items: End-to-end integration test: real Twilio call → Pipecat → Groq → Fish Audio → caller hears response
Deploy backend to cloud (Railway/Fly.io) with PostgreSQL + Redis
Configure Twilio webhook to point at deployed backend
Test with 10+ real phone calls across scenarios (known contact, spam, social engineering, legitimate unknown)
Validate security_analyzer.py against real social engineering scripts (IRS scam, tech support, bank fraud, impersonation)
Add confidence scoring and threshold tuning for false positive rate
Build evaluation dataset: 50+ labeled call transcripts (benign vs malicious)
Add real-time escalation: push notification to user when social engineering detected mid-call
Build Flutter iOS app (currently empty scaffold) — minimum: login, call log, settings, push notifications
Implement Stripe billing (free tier + $9.99/mo premium)
App Store submission: privacy policy, screenshots, review guidelines compliance
Landing page with demo video showing social engineering detection in action
Onboarding flow: Twilio number provisioning or BYOP (bring your own phone forwarding)
Latency benchmarking: measure and optimize end-to-end response time (target <800ms)
Add structured logging and call analytics dashboard
Voice quality A/B testing: Fish Audio vs ElevenLabs vs Deepgram TTS
Handle edge cases: caller hangs up mid-conversation, network drops, API timeouts
Android app or progressive web app
Multi-user support (family plan)
Custom AI personality/voice per user
Voicemail transcription and AI summary
Integration with smart home (announce caller on HomePod/Alexa)
